title: runtime: export scheduler lag + queue depth metrics for overload protection
preview: Context: Hot-route capacity protection needs a small set of runtime-originated pressure signals that are (a) cheap to compute in the data plane, (b) stable under high load, and (c) low-cardinality enough to be safely shipped at high frequency. This PR adds two primary signals to the runtime metrics pipeline: schedul...
text:
Context: Hot-route capacity protection needs a small set of runtime-originated pressure signals that are (a) cheap to compute in the data plane, (b) stable under high load, and (c) low-cardinality enough to be safely shipped at high frequency. This PR adds two primary signals to the runtime metrics pipeline: scheduler lag (time between eligible-to-run and actually scheduled) and queue depth (pending work items) with limited dimensionality.

What’s in this PR: 1) Scheduler lag histogram (ms) emitted at the scheduling boundary per engine instance. Lag is computed as (now - enqueue_ts) at the moment the request enters a runnable slot. 2) Queue depth gauge emitted from the admission queue, representing pending requests waiting for runtime scheduling. 3) Optional route_group attribution: runtime does NOT attach raw route/path; it accepts a pre-aggregated route_group identifier from the rou...

title: Enable feature-flagged hot-route protection (us-east canary) + rollback guardrails
preview:
Context
This PR wires up progressive rollout controls for Hot-route capacity protection (tier-aware admission control / priority routing) and performs the first canary enablement in us-east.

We already have the core enforcement plumbing landing in:
- Gateway tier-aware rate limiting + 429 semantics: pr-27411 / pr-2...
text:
Context
This PR wires up progressive rollout controls for Hot-route capacity protection (tier-aware admission control / priority routing) and performs the first canary enablement in us-east.

We already have the core enforcement plumbing landing in:
- Gateway tier-aware rate limiting + 429 semantics: pr-27411 / pr-27441
- Policy engine priority classes + route budgets: pr-27418
- Routing admission control using capacity signals: pr-27427
- Runtime metrics export: pr-27435

What this PR adds is the rollout substrate: feature flags, allowlists, and a repeatable enable/disable workflow with guardrails.

Goals
- Default safe: all protection behavior OFF unless explicitly enabled for a region + account allowlist.
- Canary first: enable for a small set of enterprise accounts and the protected route group in us-east.
- Reversible: provide one-command rollback that disables protection at gate...

title: Add perf-canary alert handler (Slack threads, dedupe, runbook links)
preview:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit ...
text:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit alerts as a webhook payload (model + region + tier + metric + observed vs baseline + confidence + baseline version/runtime tags). Before this PR, incident-bot treated canary payloads as generic webhooks which led to noisy, non-threaded posts and no reliable dedupe key, making it hard to triage during regressions and especially during baseline drift/threshold tuning.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new webhook route + handler for source=perf-canary (Content-Type: application/json). 2) Builds a stable dedupe key and thread key so repeat evaluations (same model/metric/region/sev...

title: Add status summary metadata to incident events
preview:
Context
We’re implementing auto-drafted, publishable post-incident summaries (ENG-4823). The incident-bot can already export a timeline of events (see PR #19540), but the events don’t currently carry the structured metadata we need to generate consistent “Impact / Timeline / Mitigation” blocks without hand-parsing f...
text:
Context
We’re implementing auto-drafted, publishable post-incident summaries (ENG-4823). The incident-bot can already export a timeline of events (see PR #19540), but the events don’t currently carry the structured metadata we need to generate consistent “Impact / Timeline / Mitigation” blocks without hand-parsing free-form notes.

This PR adds optional, structured “status summary” metadata to incident events emitted by the redwood control plane so incident-bot can draft post-incident summaries with fewer manual edits and lower risk of accidentally including sensitive detail.

What’s included
- Adds optional fields to incident event payloads:
 - impact_start (RFC3339 timestamp; when customer impact started, as determined by IC/SRE)
 - impact_end (RFC3339 timestamp; when customer impact ended / SLO recovered)
 - affected_routes (array of route identifiers, e.g. "chat.completions", "emb...

title: Anti-flap logic (windows + hysteresis) for status-component health signals
preview: Motivation: Our current component health rollups (used to recommend public status changes and to populate status-page dashboards) are too twitchy. Short-lived spikes (e.g., 2–3 minutes of elevated 5xx or a brief p95 regression) can flip a component between Operational ↔ Degraded multiple times, which creates noisy o...
text: Motivation: Our current component health rollups (used to recommend public status changes and to populate status-page dashboards) are too twitchy. Short-lived spikes (e.g., 2–3 minutes of elevated 5xx or a brief p95 regression) can flip a component between Operational ↔ Degraded multiple times, which creates noisy on-call workflows and undermines customer trust in the status page. This PR implements anti-flap windows and hysteresis in the status-signal evaluator so we only transition component state when a condition persists long enough, and we require a healthier condition to recover than the one that caused the degradation. Summary of changes: Adds a small state machine around each status signal (errors + latency) with configurable (1) trigger window (min duration / min samples) and (2) recovery window (min duration / min samples) plus (3) hysteresis thresholds (degrade_threshold vs...

title: Support approval step for Sev0/Sev1 status page publishes (optional)
preview: Context: As part of the Status page transparency upgrades initiative, we’ve standardized comms templates and added Comms Lead reminders. One gap that came up repeatedly (Support escalations + game days) is that during Sev0/Sev1 incidents we sometimes publish status updates with wording that Support has to immediatel...
text:
Context: As part of the Status page transparency upgrades initiative, we’ve standardized comms templates and added Comms Lead reminders. One gap that came up repeatedly (Support escalations + game days) is that during Sev0/Sev1 incidents we sometimes publish status updates with wording that Support has to immediately clarify, or we publish a component impact that later turns out to be wrong (especially when impact is limited to a subset of customers/regions). This PR introduces an optional approval step in incident-bot before publishing status page updates for Sev0/Sev1, allowing either (a) the on-call Incident Commander or (b) an on-call Support lead to approve the draft. This is intended to improve accuracy without adding overhead to Sev2+ events.

What’s changing:
- Adds a configurable “publish requires approval” gate that activates only for Sev0/Sev1 incidents and only when the ac...

title: Link SLO burn alerts to status page update runbook + component taxonomy
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, on-call needs a fast, consistent path from a page to (a) the correct status-page component taxonomy and (b) the exact runbook steps for posting/updating component statuses. Today, most of our burn-rate alerts include a generic runbook link (or no runbook at ...
text: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, on-call needs a fast, consistent path from a page to (a) the correct status-page component taxonomy and (b) the exact runbook steps for posting/updating component statuses. Today, most of our burn-rate alerts include a generic runbook link (or no runbook at all), and nothing points to the canonical taxonomy/mapping guidance. This PR updates alert annotations across component-level SLO burn alerts in this repo to include: (1) a status page update runbook link, and (2) a status page component taxonomy link. It also standardizes annotation keys so incident-bot and human responders can reliably surface the right links in Slack. Scope: alert rule annotations + lint/test updates only; no threshold or routing changes. Notes: we intentionally keep the links to high-level canonical docs rather than incident-specific pages, and we avoid...

title: Add KMS throttle budget + request backpressure to prevent KMS saturation under spikes
preview: Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope...
text:
Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope ops (e.g. cache cold start + traffic spike) could saturate KMS and extend the incident blast radius.

This PR introduces an explicit "KMS throttle budget" and integrates KMS calls with the existing request admission/backpressure path so we shed/queue work earlier rather than stampeding KMS.

What’s included (high level): (1) Per-process token-bucket budget for KMS operations: A lightweight budget that is decremented on KMS requests and refilled at a configured rate. When depleted, KMS calls are delayed (bounded) or rejected with a typed error depending on caller policy. Th...

title: Statuspage API client: retry/backoff + idempotency keys + audit log entries
preview: Problem: incident-bot’s Statuspage integration currently performs best-effort POST/PATCH calls with minimal retry handling. During Sev0/Sev1s we’ve seen transient 5xx/timeout/429 responses (plus occasional network blips) leading to (a) failed publishes that require manual retry and (b) duplicate updates when a human...
text: Problem: incident-bot’s Statuspage integration currently performs best-effort POST/PATCH calls with minimal retry handling. During Sev0/Sev1s we’ve seen transient 5xx/timeout/429 responses (plus occasional network blips) leading to (a) failed publishes that require manual retry and (b) duplicate updates when a human re-triggers a step while the original request is still in flight. This PR introduces a small Statuspage API client wrapper with standardized retry/backoff, idempotency keys, and structured audit log entries for every attempted publish/update. Scope: add an HTTP client with (1) bounded retries for retryable failures (429, 5xx, network timeouts), (2) exponential backoff with jitter and respect for Retry-After where present, (3) deterministic idempotency keys so retries and replays don’t create duplicate incidents/updates, and (4) audit logging to our internal incident-bot au...

title: Add overload shedding dashboards + initial alert rules (breaker opens, shed rate)
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program, we need first-class visibility into (a) when we are shedding traffic vs failing implicitly, and (b) when breakers are opening/half-opening/closing across regions/models/tiers. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and initial Prometheus a...
text: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program, we need first-class visibility into (a) when we are shedding traffic vs failing implicitly, and (b) when breakers are opening/half-opening/closing across regions/models/tiers. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and initial Prometheus alert rules for overload protection signals. Scope: (1) Dashboards: Add "Hosted API • Overload Protection" dashboard group with panels for shed rate, breaker open rate, admission decisions, runtime saturation proxies (queue depth / token saturation / memory pressure proxy), and top shed reasons. Includes recommended drilldowns by region and route family, and a safe set of labels (bounded cardinality). (2) Alerts: Add first-pass paging + ticket alerts for (i) sustained breaker-open rate, (ii) elevated shed rate for paid tiers, (iii) Retry-After header missing on overload resp...

title: Smart Routing: fallback model/region when overload breaker is open (bounded + loop-safe)
preview:
Context: As part of the hosted overload protection work, we want to degrade gracefully instead of cascading timeouts when a model/region is saturated. This PR adds a routing-side fallback attempt when the upstream admission layer reports an overload breaker open (or equivalent overload classification).

What this PR...
text:
Context: As part of the hosted overload protection work, we want to degrade gracefully instead of cascading timeouts when a model/region is saturated. This PR adds a routing-side fallback attempt when the upstream admission layer reports an overload breaker open (or equivalent overload classification).

What this PR does: Implements a fallback resolution step in Smart Routing that can (optionally) route to a compatible fallback model and/or fallback region based on tenant + route policy. Adds strict loop prevention so we don’t bounce between model variants/regions, and bounds fallback attempts to a small fixed number to avoid amplifying load. Also ensures we do not fallback for non-overload failures (e.g., auth, invalid params, quota exceeded).

Key behaviors: (1) Trigger conditions: only when overload classification indicates breaker open / load shed / capacity unavailable (see mappi...

title: Generate Statuspage update drafts from incident state (phase templates + metrics)
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we want to reduce time-to-first-update and improve consistency across incident phases without increasing on-call burden. This PR adds a draft generator that produces templated Statuspage update text based on the current incident state (Investigating/Identifi...
text:
Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we want to reduce time-to-first-update and improve consistency across incident phases without increasing on-call burden. This PR adds a draft generator that produces templated Statuspage update text based on the current incident state (Investigating/Identified/Mitigating/Monitoring/Resolved). Drafts pull in the structured fields we already collect at intake (component/region/capability) plus optional impact metrics and known mitigations. The output is a DRAFT only (explicitly labeled) and requires a human publish step.

What this does: Adds a template registry for each incident phase with a small variable set (component_display, regions, capabilities, started_at, current_state, impact_summary, error_rate, p95_latency_ms, affected_routes, customer_visible_symptoms, mitigations_in_progress, fallback_active, next_update_eta). Add...

title: Announce circuit breaker state transitions to #incidents and #eng-oncall
preview:
Context
As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding program (ENG-4821), we need a low-latency, human-visible signal when breakers start shedding (OPEN) and when they recover (HALF_OPEN/CLOSED). The current workflow relies on dashboards/alerts only; during incidents this increases time-to-diagnosis and con...
text:
Context
As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding program (ENG-4821), we need a low-latency, human-visible signal when breakers start shedding (OPEN) and when they recover (HALF_OPEN/CLOSED). The current workflow relies on dashboards/alerts only; during incidents this increases time-to-diagnosis and contributes to duplicate triage.

This PR teaches incident-bot to announce breaker state transitions into Slack (#incidents and #eng-oncall) with:
- Transition summary (CLOSED → OPEN, OPEN → HALF_OPEN, HALF_OPEN → CLOSED)
- Scope (region, route, model selector, tenant/tier when available)
- Top contributing signal(s) (queue depth, token/sec saturation, dependency health)
- Links to dashboards and the oncall runbook
- A short “recommended actions” block aligned with the runbook

Behavior
- Posts OPEN transitions to both #incidents and #eng-oncall.
- Posts recovery transitions (...

title: Add component-level SLO dashboards + standard links for status page components
preview: Context: As part of the Status Page Transparency Upgrades initiative, on-call needs a consistent, component-level view of latency + error impact that matches the public status component taxonomy (component_id x region x capability). Today dashboards are a mix of service names (gateway/scheduler/streamer), inconsiste...
text: Context: As part of the Status Page Transparency Upgrades initiative, on-call needs a consistent, component-level view of latency + error impact that matches the public status component taxonomy (component_id x region x capability). Today dashboards are a mix of service names (gateway/scheduler/streamer), inconsistent region labels, and ad-hoc links in runbooks. This PR adds a standardized dashboard set per status component and a link registry so alerts/runbooks/status tooling can point to the same canonical URLs. What’s in this PR: (1) New Grafana dashboards for each public status component, split into latency + errors with consistent panels and templating. (2) Standard naming conventions for dashboards, folders, variables, and panel titles (to reduce “is this the right chart?” time during Sev1). (3) A generated link map (component_id/region/capability -> dashboard URL) committed und...

title: Add JSON schema + CI validation for status component registry
preview: This PR introduces a JSON Schema for the public status-page component registry and adds validation in CI so registry changes fail fast when they are malformed or violate taxonomy rules. Motivation: we’re using the registry as a source-of-truth for component ids/display names/ownership and rollout flags as part of th...
text: This PR introduces a JSON Schema for the public status-page component registry and adds validation in CI so registry changes fail fast when they are malformed or violate taxonomy rules. Motivation: we’re using the registry as a source-of-truth for component ids/display names/ownership and rollout flags as part of the Status page transparency upgrades effort; recent incidents and hotfixes showed that small typos (ids, missing fields, inconsistent region/capability labels) can cause misclassification and customer-facing status inaccuracies. What’s included: (1) New schema file `statuspage/component-registry.schema.json` that defines required fields and constraints for each component entry: `component_id` (kebab-case, stable, unique), `display_name` (human readable), `product_area` (hosted_api|streaming|embeddings|reranking|console|dedicated_control_plane), `regions` (allowed set), `capa...

title: gateway: add admission control middleware (token bucket + concurrency caps) wired to overload policy engine
preview:
Title: gateway: add admission control middleware (token bucket + concurrency caps) wired to overload policy engine

Context
We’re starting Phase 1 for Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding (ENG-4821). A recurring failure mode in incidents is the gateway allowing bursts to fully propagate to routing/runtime, which ...
text:
Title: gateway: add admission control middleware (token bucket + concurrency caps) wired to overload policy engine

Context
We’re starting Phase 1 for Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding (ENG-4821). A recurring failure mode in incidents is the gateway allowing bursts to fully propagate to routing/runtime, which creates tail-latency blowups, timeouts, and retry storms.

This PR adds a first admission-control layer in the API gateway with:
- Token bucket rate limiting (RPS) keyed by tenant + route (+ optional model selector)
- Concurrency caps (in-flight requests) keyed by tenant + route (with separate buckets for streaming vs non-streaming)
- Policy evaluation via the overload policy engine (tenant overrides + tier defaults), including a safe-mode fallback when the policy engine is unavailable
- Metrics for “allowed vs rejected” and limiter state (designed to keep cardinality bound...

title: hotfix(kms): reduce retry amplification on timeouts + add jitter
preview: Context: We had a near-miss on 2025-11-16 where transient KMS timeouts caused a retry storm and elevated 5xx due to retry amplification across layers (gateway -> control-plane client -> kms wrapper). This PR is a production hotfix to reduce amplification, introduce jitter, and ensure we respect an end-to-end retry b...
text:
Context: We had a near-miss on 2025-11-16 where transient KMS timeouts caused a retry storm and elevated 5xx due to retry amplification across layers (gateway -> control-plane client -> kms wrapper). This PR is a production hotfix to reduce amplification, introduce jitter, and ensure we respect an end-to-end retry budget so we don’t turn a dependency blip into a load event. Incident thread: slack://incidents/1747779033-near-miss-kms-retry-storm (related earlier spike: slack://incidents/1746508855-kms-timeout-spike-us-east). Customer correlation: SUP-19377.

What changed (high level): 1) Enforce a single retry budget per request for KMS decrypt/envelope operations (attempts + time). 2) Switch timeout retry backoff to exponential w/ full jitter (AWS recommended) and cap max sleep. 3) Stop retrying once the call is past the request deadline (previously we could start a new attempt with <...

title: Incident intake: add required status page component/region/capability fields + validation
preview: Why: We keep losing time (and occasionally accuracy) in the first 10–15 minutes of an incident because the intake form doesn’t capture the info required to pick the right public status page component(s). This led to coarse updates ("API") even when the impact was limited to Streaming or Embeddings in a single region...
text:
Why: We keep losing time (and occasionally accuracy) in the first 10–15 minutes of an incident because the intake form doesn’t capture the info required to pick the right public status page component(s). This led to coarse updates ("API") even when the impact was limited to Streaming or Embeddings in a single region. This PR updates the incident intake workflow to require three customer-facing classification fields (component, region, capability) and adds validation + reminders so Comms Lead can publish component-accurate updates quickly.

What changed (high level): - Adds new required intake fields: status_component_id, impacted_region, impacted_capability - Adds opinionated defaults + "Unknown/TBD" escape hatch to avoid blocking declaration - Adds server-side validation for required fields on incident creation + updates - Writes selected values into the incident record (used later b...

title: Add Comms Lead role assignment + TTFU reminders; log status updates to #incidents
preview: Context: We’ve had repeated incidents where we miss the first customer-facing update window (TTFU), and ownership of incident communications is ambiguous when the IC is deep in mitigation. This PR adds an explicit Comms Lead role to the incident-bot workflow, adds configurable reminders tied to severity (TTFU target...
text: Context: We’ve had repeated incidents where we miss the first customer-facing update window (TTFU), and ownership of incident communications is ambiguous when the IC is deep in mitigation. This PR adds an explicit Comms Lead role to the incident-bot workflow, adds configurable reminders tied to severity (TTFU targets), and logs customer-facing update actions to #incidents for shared situational awareness and later audits. Changes: (1) Incident intake now captures/assigns a Comms Lead (default: secondary responder if present; else falls back to IC; can be reassigned). (2) Bot posts a lightweight “TTFU reminder” in the incident Slack channel if no status update has been logged within the target window (Sev0/Sev1 only by default). (3) Any status update action initiated via bot commands is echoed to #incidents with incident link + component(s) + state (Investigating/Identified/etc.) and t...

title: Add overload error classification rules (separate from 5xx) for Hosted API SLO reporting
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the gateway and downstream services now emit stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 + a Redwood error code + Retry-After). Our current SLO classifier treats most 5xx as availability failures, which makes overload-induced shedding look like an ...
text:
Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the gateway and downstream services now emit stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 + a Redwood error code + Retry-After). Our current SLO classifier treats most 5xx as availability failures, which makes overload-induced shedding look like an outage and obscures the intent of graceful degradation. This PR updates slo-toolkit classification rules so overload errors are tracked separately from generic 5xx while still being visible in dashboards/alerts.

What this changes: Adds a new classifier bucket `overload` and rules to map requests into it based on (1) the canonical Redwood error code (from `x-redwood-error-code` header or JSON body `error.code`) and (2) the overload HTTP status mapping (429/503 with overload code). Removes these responses from the default 5xx classifier so they no longer inflate `server_erro...

title: Hotfix: fix misclassified embeddings errors in status component map
preview:
Context
We discovered during a recent degradation + subsequent support escalations that embeddings request failures were being attributed to the Chat component in our public status taxonomy.

Impact
- Status automation and on-call updates were marking "Hosted API (Chat)" as degraded when the majority of errors were ...
text:
Context
We discovered during a recent degradation + subsequent support escalations that embeddings request failures were being attributed to the Chat component in our public status taxonomy.

Impact
- Status automation and on-call updates were marking "Hosted API (Chat)" as degraded when the majority of errors were isolated to embeddings.
- "Hosted API (Embeddings)" could remain green even when /v1/embeddings was returning elevated 5xx (misleading for customers and Support).
- This also polluted the weekly status accuracy audit (component_id mismatch between incident tags and observed error distribution).

Root cause
The component classifier in the gateway error taxonomy used an ordered list of matchers. The chat matcher was too broad and was evaluated before the embeddings matcher. In particular:
- The chat matcher included a path prefix pattern that matched both /v1/chat/* and /v1/e...

title: Policy engine: overload rules, schema validation, and safe default tier policies (+ tenant config migration)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds first-class overload protection rules to the policy engine, introduces strict schema validation (server-side) with safe defaults, and includes a one-time migration for existing tenant configs to the new `overload_policies` stanza.

## Motivation
As part of Hosted circuit breakers + load shedd...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds first-class overload protection rules to the policy engine, introduces strict schema validation (server-side) with safe defaults, and includes a one-time migration for existing tenant configs to the new `overload_policies` stanza.

## Motivation
As part of Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding (ENG-4821), the gateway and router need a single source of truth for tenant/tier/model-level overload behavior (admission caps, shed behavior, Retry-After hints, and optional degradation flags). We also need to prevent unsafe or foot-gun configurations (e.g., disabling shedding globally or setting unlimited concurrency for self-serve tiers) while still allowing per-tenant overrides for enterprise.

## What’s included
- New rule types under `overload_policies.v1`:
 - `admission_control`: token bucket + concurrency caps (per route/model selector)
 - `load_shedding`: shed ...

title: Add KMS dependency healthcheck endpoint + SRE hooks (predeploy gate)
preview:
Summary
- Adds an internal KMS dependency healthcheck endpoint for services that rely on envelope decryption.
- Exposes standardized SRE hooks so rollout tooling can gate deploys on KMS readiness and SRE can alert on sustained degradation.

Motivation / context
We’ve had a few cases (and a recent us-east timeout spi...
text:
Summary
- Adds an internal KMS dependency healthcheck endpoint for services that rely on envelope decryption.
- Exposes standardized SRE hooks so rollout tooling can gate deploys on KMS readiness and SRE can alert on sustained degradation.

Motivation / context
We’ve had a few cases (and a recent us-east timeout spike) where KMS latency/throttling started degrading request paths, but we didn’t have a single, low-noise signal that answers: “is KMS healthy enough for this service to safely take traffic right now?”

This PR implements an internal healthcheck that:
- does NOT call KMS on each request (avoids amplification / self-inflicted throttling)
- distinguishes transient vs sustained failures
- standardizes output so alerting + predeploy checks can consume it consistently

Design highlights
- Endpoint: GET /internal/health/kms
 - Returns 200 when KMS is healthy per local probe state
...

title: Add burst traffic scenario to validate overload shedding vs timeouts
preview: Summary: This PR adds a new perf-canary scenario that generates bursty, highly-variable request load (concurrency ramp + prompt-length ramp) against Hosted API routes to validate overload protection behavior. The goal is to ensure we see explicit overload responses (429/503 w/ stable error codes + Retry-After) and g...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a new perf-canary scenario that generates bursty, highly-variable request load (concurrency ramp + prompt-length ramp) against Hosted API routes to validate overload protection behavior. The goal is to ensure we see explicit overload responses (429/503 w/ stable error codes + Retry-After) and graceful degradation instead of tail-latency blowups/timeouts during bursts.

Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program (ENG-4821), we need a repeatable canary that exercises the failure mode we see in real incidents: fast concurrency spikes plus long prompts causing prefill pressure and queue backlogs. We previously only had steady-state throughput scenarios, which are not good at catching retry storms/timeouts. This scenario is intended to be used in dark launch + regional canary gates.

What’s included: 1) New scenario: burst_over...

title: Add standardized overload error codes + JSON payload (Hosted API)
preview: Context: During overload we currently surface a mix of upstream timeouts, generic 503s, and ad-hoc JSON bodies depending on which hop rejects the request (gateway vs routing vs runtime admission). That makes client behavior inconsistent and increases retry storms (clients retry immediately on timeouts/unknown 5xx). ...
text:
Context: During overload we currently surface a mix of upstream timeouts, generic 503s, and ad-hoc JSON bodies depending on which hop rejects the request (gateway vs routing vs runtime admission). That makes client behavior inconsistent and increases retry storms (clients retry immediately on timeouts/unknown 5xx). This PR introduces a single canonical overload error response struct and a stable error-code taxonomy for Hosted API overload/load-shedding. Goal is to make overload failures explicit, machine-parseable, and consistent across the request path while remaining backwards compatible (additive fields only). This aligns with ADR-014 (Hosted overload error taxonomy) and sets up follow-on work in the gateway middleware + routing fallback (ENG-4823/ENG-4824).

What changed: 1) Add `OverloadErrorResponse` (shared API error type) with stable fields: `error.type=overload_error`, `error...

title: Add incident timeline export hook for post-incident summary generation
preview: Context: As part of the Status Page Transparency Upgrades work, we need a reliable, structured export of incident timeline events (state changes, mitigations, key decisions) so we can auto-draft publishable post-incident summaries and run accuracy audits. Today the incident service stores the timeline in the DB and ...
text:
Context: As part of the Status Page Transparency Upgrades work, we need a reliable, structured export of incident timeline events (state changes, mitigations, key decisions) so we can auto-draft publishable post-incident summaries and run accuracy audits. Today the incident service stores the timeline in the DB and renders it in the internal UI, but there is no stable export contract and downstream consumers have been scraping. This PR introduces a first-class export hook in the `incident-api` service that emits a canonical stream of timeline events and exposes a read API for the summary generator.

What changed: (1) Added a new `IncidentTimelineEventV1` schema with a stable set of fields (incident_id, event_id, occurred_at, actor, event_type, state_from/state_to, summary, details_redacted, tags). (2) Added an append-only export hook that publishes events to `incident.timeline.v1` via...
links: Linear ENG-4830 (Map internal services to status components)

title: Add KMS latency/error dashboards + alert rules (per env/region)
preview: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext...
text: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext/Unknown) and our metric namespacing conventions. Scope: (1) Dashboard: Overview + per env/region panels for request volume, latency (p50/p95/p99), error rate by category, throttling signals, and circuit breaker state; (2) Alerts: page-worthy vs ticket-worthy thresholds, with environment-aware routing (prod pages, staging tickets) and burn-rate style windows where appropriate; (3) Operator ergonomics: runbook links + log/trace pivot hints (trace_id, kms_key_id, provider, region, service); (4) Documentation: short README explaining how to use/tune the alerts and what to chec...

title: Implement KMS circuit breaker + fallback mode to prevent retry storms during KMS incidents
preview: Context: During recent KMS throttling/timeouts (see SUP-19377), application-side retries amplified load and created a request storm against KMS, worsening tail latency and elevating 5xx. We’ve already standardized timeouts/retries in the KMS wrapper (PR #18421); this change adds circuit breaking + a documented degra...
text:
Context: During recent KMS throttling/timeouts (see SUP-19377), application-side retries amplified load and created a request storm against KMS, worsening tail latency and elevating 5xx. We’ve already standardized timeouts/retries in the KMS wrapper (PR #18421); this change adds circuit breaking + a documented degrade/fallback policy so we fail fast and shed load instead of stampeding KMS.

What this PR does:
- Adds a per-key (key ARN) circuit breaker in the shared KMS client wrapper with CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN states.
- Introduces a fallback mode policy for decrypt/envelope operations:
 - If circuit is OPEN: do not call KMS; attempt to serve from in-memory DEK cache if present (bounded TTL, non-persistent); otherwise fail fast with a mapped KMSUnavailable error (no retries).
 - Encrypt/new key generation paths: fail fast when OPEN (no cache fallback), returning a distinct error class ...

title: Add integration test harness for KMS failure modes (timeouts/throttling) + retry/latency assertions
preview: Context: As part of the KMS hardening initiative (ENG-4821/ENG-4825) we need repeatable, non-flaky coverage for the most common real-world failure modes we’ve seen (timeouts and throttling) and guardrails to prevent retry amplification. This PR introduces an integration-test harness that can deterministically inject...
text: Context: As part of the KMS hardening initiative (ENG-4821/ENG-4825) we need repeatable, non-flaky coverage for the most common real-world failure modes we’ve seen (timeouts and throttling) and guardrails to prevent retry amplification. This PR introduces an integration-test harness that can deterministically inject KMS failures and validate that our KMS wrapper obeys configured budgets (max retries + max wall-clock) and that upstream services surface the correct error category without masking (e.g., Throttling vs Timeout vs AccessDenied). What’s included: (1) A small “kms-fault-injector” sidecar used only in integration tests (runs in docker-compose and the CI integration job). It proxies KMS requests and can inject (a) fixed latency, (b) probabilistic timeouts (connection stall), and (c) throttling responses (AWS-style ThrottlingException / HTTP 429 equivalent) based on per-test con...

title: Add incident template/playbook for Provision-to-Ready (GPU provisioning) failures
preview:
## Summary
Adds a dedicated incident template + lightweight playbook for Provision-to-Ready / Dedicated fleet bring-up failures (nodes stuck in Provisioning/Validating, mass quarantine, image/driver mismatch, join failures, NCCL smoke flakes).

This is intended to standardize the minimum info we capture in #incident...
text:
## Summary
Adds a dedicated incident template + lightweight playbook for Provision-to-Ready / Dedicated fleet bring-up failures (nodes stuck in Provisioning/Validating, mass quarantine, image/driver mismatch, join failures, NCCL smoke flakes).

This is intended to standardize the minimum info we capture in #incidents and in the incident doc so SRE/Infra/Platform can triage quickly and we can compute time-to-ready and failure-rate impacts without back-and-forth.

## Why
During the staged rollout we saw a few common patterns:
- Missing pool metadata (region/GPU type/image channel), which slowed down correlating failures to a specific rollout.
- Unclear ownership handoff (Infra vs Platform vs SRE) depending on whether the failure is “can’t join cluster” vs “validation gate failing” vs “readiness service/API regressions”.
- Links to dashboards/runbooks living in different places and being...

title: Quarantine nodes on repeat validation failures (safe defaults + operator override)
preview:
## Summary
This PR implements automatic quarantine for nodes that repeatedly fail provision-to-ready validation. The goal is to prevent noisy/flaky nodes from looping forever in Validating and then accidentally joining serving pools, while keeping defaults conservative and providing explicit operator override pathwa...
text:
## Summary
This PR implements automatic quarantine for nodes that repeatedly fail provision-to-ready validation. The goal is to prevent noisy/flaky nodes from looping forever in Validating and then accidentally joining serving pools, while keeping defaults conservative and providing explicit operator override pathways.

## Motivation / Context
During staged rollout we saw a subset of nodes repeatedly failing the same validation step (most commonly: driver parity drift, GPU health/Xid, and NCCL smoke failures). Retrying indefinitely increases time-to-ready, consumes orchestrator capacity, and creates ambiguous operator outcomes ("is this a transient flake or a bad node?").

This change adds:
- A canonical definition of "repeat validation failure" (consecutive failures of the same validation category within a time window)
- Automatic transition to `Quarantined` after the threshold is ex...

title: Add time-to-ready SLI calculation (provisioning -> Ready) from event timestamps
preview:
Title: Add time-to-ready SLI calculation (provisioning -> Ready) from event timestamps

Context
We’re rolling out provision-to-ready automation for Dedicated pools, and we need a canonical SLI for “time-to-ready” that is consistent across regions/GPU types and robust to retries/quarantine. This PR adds an SLI calcul...
text:
Title: Add time-to-ready SLI calculation (provisioning -> Ready) from event timestamps

Context
We’re rolling out provision-to-ready automation for Dedicated pools, and we need a canonical SLI for “time-to-ready” that is consistent across regions/GPU types and robust to retries/quarantine. This PR adds an SLI calculator in slo-toolkit that derives duration from the event stream produced by the provisioning orchestrator and fleet readiness service.

What this PR does
- Introduces a new SLI: time_to_ready_seconds (aka provision_to_ready_seconds) computed per node instance based on event timestamps.
- Uses event timestamps rather than scrape-time to avoid skew and to survive delayed ingestion.
- Defines the start and end markers as:
 - start: first Observed(Allocated) or ProvisioningStarted event for a node (prefers Allocated when present)
 - end: first Ready event (terminal Ready transi...

title: Add Private observability smoke-test harness (kind + Helm) and gate on core signals
preview: Motivation: We keep finding regressions in Private installs where Prometheus scrapes succeed but dashboards/rules don’t render (missing ConfigMaps), or metrics rename breaks the minimum pack. This PR adds a deterministic smoke-test harness that deploys a minimal Private stack into a fresh kind cluster and asserts th...
text:
Motivation: We keep finding regressions in Private installs where Prometheus scrapes succeed but dashboards/rules don’t render (missing ConfigMaps), or metrics rename breaks the minimum pack. This PR adds a deterministic smoke-test harness that deploys a minimal Private stack into a fresh kind cluster and asserts the observability minimum pack is actually usable (scrapes + basic PromQL + dashboards + alert rules). This is intended to be the v1 gate for ENG-19156 and to prevent repeats of INT-11804 failures and SUP-49210-style issues.

What this adds: (1) `hack/private_observability_smoke/` harness that creates a kind cluster, installs dependencies (kube-prometheus-stack + grafana), installs Redwood Private via Helm (control-plane + gateway + one runtime deployment), then validates signals; (2) a Go-based validator binary that queries Prometheus HTTP API and checks for presence of expe...

title: Add structured logging fields + redaction safeguards for Private troubleshooting
preview:
Title: Add structured logging fields + redaction safeguards for Private troubleshooting

Context
Private customers and Support/SRE need a reliable way to correlate failures across the gateway, control plane, and serving runtime without requiring payload logging or environment-specific bespoke parsing. Today, several...
text:
Title: Add structured logging fields + redaction safeguards for Private troubleshooting

Context
Private customers and Support/SRE need a reliable way to correlate failures across the gateway, control plane, and serving runtime without requiring payload logging or environment-specific bespoke parsing. Today, several components log different key names (or omit them entirely), and a few code paths still allow unredacted headers to slip into debug logs.

This PR aligns the log schema for Private installs with the minimum pack expectations and adds redaction safeguards suitable for regulated environments.

Summary of changes
1) Standardized structured fields (JSON logs)
- request_id: propagated from API gateway to downstream control-plane calls and runtime request handling. If an inbound X-Request-Id is present, we honor it (bounded length + charset validation); otherwise generate a UUIDv...

title: Standardize metric labels and reduce cardinality for Private installs
preview: Context: Private installs run customer-managed Prometheus with tighter TSDB/storage limits and stricter cardinality constraints than our hosted environments. We observed high series churn from per-request and per-route/model labels, especially when customers have many routes, frequently changing model versions, or m...
text: Context: Private installs run customer-managed Prometheus with tighter TSDB/storage limits and stricter cardinality constraints than our hosted environments. We observed high series churn from per-request and per-route/model labels, especially when customers have many routes, frequently changing model versions, or multi-tenant traffic. This PR standardizes label names across control plane + data plane services and introduces bounded label strategies for Private (while keeping a compatibility path for existing dashboards/alerts). Scope: (1) Label normalization: unify common label keys across services (service/component/namespace/cluster), rename inconsistent keys (route vs endpoint, model vs model_name), and remove labels that should never exist on metrics (request_id, raw path). (2) Cardinality reduction for Private: replace unbounded labels with bounded dimensions: route_group (confi...

title: Add Private minimum pack Prometheus alert rules: latency + availability (with SLO burn recording rules)
preview: Context: This PR introduces the baseline alerting + recording rules for the Redwood Private observability minimum pack, focused on the highest-impact failure modes for inference availability and latency regressions. These are intended to be safe-by-default in customer clusters (bounded cardinality, conservative pagi...
text:
Context: This PR introduces the baseline alerting + recording rules for the Redwood Private observability minimum pack, focused on the highest-impact failure modes for inference availability and latency regressions. These are intended to be safe-by-default in customer clusters (bounded cardinality, conservative paging) and to map cleanly to the SLO guidance we’ve been using in staging.

What’s included (v1):
- Recording rules (namespace: redwood_private:*) used by dashboards + alerts so queries are consistent and cheaper.
- Latency SLO burn alerts (multi-window, multi-burn) for the API gateway and runtime request path.
- Availability alerts (5xx rate + blackbox reachability) for API ingress and control plane critical services.
- Alert annotations include runbook + first steps links and a canonical dashboard deep link placeholder.
- promtool tests for rule syntax + basic evaluation fix...

title: Add Private minimum pack: versioned Grafana dashboard bundle (runtime + control plane + infra)
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first versioned Grafana dashboard bundle for the Private Observability Minimum Pack. The dashboards are designed to be shipped as immutable artifacts (checked into this repo) and later installed via `redwood-helm-charts`/`redwood-private-installer` (follow-ups). text: Summary: This PR adds the first versioned Grafana dashboard bundle for the Private Observability Minimum Pack. The dashboards are designed to be shipped as immutable artifacts (checked into this repo) and later installed via `redwood-helm-charts`/`redwood-private-installer` (follow-ups). Scope: 1) Runtime overview dashboard focused on golden signals + inference-specific panels (token throughput, batching, KV cache, GPU utilization). 2) Control plane health dashboard (gateway/auth/policy/config services) with standard service golden signals and dependency panels (DB/redis if present). 3) Infra dashboard for customer cluster basics required to triage most common Private incidents (node pressure, PVC pressure, pod restart loops, scrape status). Design notes / constraints: - Private environments require bounded label cardinality; dashboards avoid templating on high-cardinality labels like...

title: Add alert rules + routing for Dedicated max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup
preview:
Title: Add alert rules + routing for Dedicated max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup

Context
Dedicated autoscaling bounds are rolling out to pilot customers. During pilot we’ve seen two recurring failure/impact modes that are easy to miss without dedicated alerting:
1) Deployment hits max bound for an ex...
text:
Title: Add alert rules + routing for Dedicated max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup

Context
Dedicated autoscaling bounds are rolling out to pilot customers. During pilot we’ve seen two recurring failure/impact modes that are easy to miss without dedicated alerting:
1) Deployment hits max bound for an extended period while demand continues increasing (customers see queueing / p95 latency regressions; support tickets like SUP-28411).
2) Sustained queue buildup (even if not max-bound) indicating a scaling issue, forecast signal outage, or model-level throughput regression.

This PR adds:
- Prometheus alert rules for max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup.
- Alertmanager routing to Dedicated on-call (page) vs #eng-infra (ticket/notify) depending on severity and duration.
- Rule annotations/runbook links aligned with the Dedicated autoscaler troubleshooting runbook.
...

title: Add canary scenarios for predictive autoscaling (traffic ramps + periodic spikes)
preview:
## Summary
Adds two new perf-canary workloads intended to catch latency regressions and oscillation when Dedicated predictive autoscaling is enabled:
1) `predictive_ramp_30m`: steady baseline then a monotonic ramp (to mimic “daily ramp” customers).
2) `predictive_spikes_10m`: periodic spikes with short recovery wind...
text:
## Summary
Adds two new perf-canary workloads intended to catch latency regressions and oscillation when Dedicated predictive autoscaling is enabled:
1) `predictive_ramp_30m`: steady baseline then a monotonic ramp (to mimic “daily ramp” customers).
2) `predictive_spikes_10m`: periodic spikes with short recovery windows (to mimic bursty SaaS/cron-driven traffic).

These scenarios run against a Dedicated deployment with predictive mode toggled via canary config (feature-flagged per account) and record:
- p50/p95/p99 end-to-end latency
- queue depth (when available)
- autoscaler desired vs bounded desired replicas (to detect max-bound saturation and clamp behavior)
- scale event reasons + step sizes (to detect oscillation)
- prediction freshness + error (if forecast provider is enabled in the canary env)

The goal is to gate pilot expansions: predictive mode should not regress p95 during...

title: Dedicated autoscaler: add metrics + structured logs for desired vs bounded capacity and prediction error
preview: Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired rep...
text:
Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired replicas vs bounded desired replicas, (2) bound-hit counters (clamped to min/max), (3) prediction error and staleness when predictive mode is enabled, and (4) an explicit scale-event reason enum that shows up in both metrics and logs.

What’s in this PR:
- New gauge metrics (emitted every autoscaler tick per deployment/region):
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.desired_replicas (the raw desired output from the decision function)
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bounded_desired_replicas (after applying min/max + safety floors)
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.current_replicas
 - re...

title: Private minimum pack: add GPU saturation + OOM/resource pressure alerts for inference pods
preview: Context: Private minimum pack needs a minimal, supported set of alerts that catch the highest-impact inference failure modes (GPU saturation, GPU memory pressure, OOM kills, restart loops) without overloading customer on-calls. This PR adds GPU/resource pressure alert rules scoped to Redwood inference pods and wires...
text:
Context: Private minimum pack needs a minimal, supported set of alerts that catch the highest-impact inference failure modes (GPU saturation, GPU memory pressure, OOM kills, restart loops) without overloading customer on-calls. This PR adds GPU/resource pressure alert rules scoped to Redwood inference pods and wires them into the existing private rules bundle. It includes tuning notes from the alert-noise review and enforces label/cardinality constraints for customer Prometheus.

What’s included: (1) GPU saturation alerts (warning/ticket): sustained high GPU utilization on inference pods aggregated by cluster/namespace/workload; (2) GPU memory pressure (warning): high framebuffer utilization (FB used/total) using DCGM metrics when available; (3) OOM kill alerts (critical/page): repeated OOMKilled terminations for redwood-runtime containers over a short window; (4) restart loop alerts ...

title: Add Private upgrade health dashboards (prechecks, migrations, error rates, rollbacks)
preview: Summary: Adds a Grafana dashboard pack for Redwood Private upgrade health, focused on the new installer-driven upgrade workflow (prechecks -> backup -> control plane rollout -> data plane rollout -> migrations -> postchecks, with rollback entry points). Dashboard set covers (1) precheck failure rates + breakdown by ...
text: Summary: Adds a Grafana dashboard pack for Redwood Private upgrade health, focused on the new installer-driven upgrade workflow (prechecks -> backup -> control plane rollout -> data plane rollout -> migrations -> postchecks, with rollback entry points). Dashboard set covers (1) precheck failure rates + breakdown by check_id/severity, (2) DB migration durations and timeouts/lock waits, (3) API/control-plane error rates during upgrade windows, and (4) rollback frequency + outcomes. Motivation: SRE and Release Eng asked for a single place to validate upgrade readiness and spot regressions during beta rollouts; today we rely on scattered logs and ad-hoc queries. This PR makes upgrades observable with consistent panels and variables that work in Hosted VPC Private and on-prem (including air-gapped) where only Prometheus + Loki are available. What’s included: - New dashboard: "Private Upgra...

title: Annotate upgrade-window incidents + link to Private release artifacts
preview:
## Summary
When a Private upgrade is running (VPC or on-prem), we want incidents that fire in that window to be clearly labeled and automatically correlated to the exact release artifacts involved.

This PR updates incident-bot’s enrichment pipeline to:
- detect upgrade windows based on a canonical source (release t...
text:
## Summary
When a Private upgrade is running (VPC or on-prem), we want incidents that fire in that window to be clearly labeled and automatically correlated to the exact release artifacts involved.

This PR updates incident-bot’s enrichment pipeline to:
- detect upgrade windows based on a canonical source (release train + installer upgrade state signals)
- tag incidents created during those windows as upgrade-related
- attach links to relevant release artifacts (release tag, build provenance, and installer plan bundle) so on-call can quickly determine whether a spike is likely upgrade-induced vs. product regression.

This is part of the "Private upgrade + rollback mechanism" workstream focused on auditability and reduced MTTR.

## Motivation
We’ve repeatedly had to manually correlate incidents during maintenance windows:
- Was there an upgrade running?
- Which Private version / chart ...

title: Add internal support debug endpoint to explain burst admission decisions (redacted)
preview:
## Summary
Adds an internal-only debug endpoint + lightweight CLI tooling to help Support/SRE explain *why* a Dedicated burst request was admitted/denied, with deterministic inputs captured and safe redaction.

This is intended to reduce back-and-forth on tickets like SUP-28411 (“denials higher than expected”) and S...
text:
## Summary
Adds an internal-only debug endpoint + lightweight CLI tooling to help Support/SRE explain *why* a Dedicated burst request was admitted/denied, with deterministic inputs captured and safe redaction.

This is intended to reduce back-and-forth on tickets like SUP-28411 (“denials higher than expected”) and SUP-28503 (“429s when burst cap reached”) by providing a structured, consistent explanation including: policy snapshot, computed limits, fairness state, relevant fleet safety gates, and the final denial reason code.

## What’s included
- **Internal endpoint (mTLS + RBAC only):** `POST /internal/v1/burst/admission/explain`
 - Accepts either a `request_id` (preferred) or a minimal request envelope (tenant + region + hw_class + model + tokens_requested + is_burst + timestamp).
 - Returns a redacted explanation object:
 - decision: admitted|denied|unknown
 - denial_reason_code (...

title: Dedicated burst: add priority tiers (gold/silver/standard) with anti-starvation constraints
preview: Context: Dedicated burst pools are shared across multiple tenants (within a region/hardware class). We already enforce per-tenant burst caps + cooldowns + a pool-level safety cap, but we need an explicit tiering model for Enterprise accounts (e.g., Gold/Silver) so we can preferentially admit burst while still protec...
text:
Context: Dedicated burst pools are shared across multiple tenants (within a region/hardware class). We already enforce per-tenant burst caps + cooldowns + a pool-level safety cap, but we need an explicit tiering model for Enterprise accounts (e.g., Gold/Silver) so we can preferentially admit burst while still protecting baseline tenants and preventing starvation.

This PR adds Priority Tiers to the burst admission controller fairness layer. The tier affects the tenant’s effective burst token refill rate / weight when the shared pool is contended.

Key points:
- Adds `priority_tier` to tenant burst config: `STANDARD | SILVER | GOLD`.
- Implements weighted fairness in the admission controller using tier weights (default weights: STANDARD=1.0, SILVER=1.5, GOLD=2.0) but with hard constraints:
 - Anti-starvation floor: every enabled tenant gets a minimum refill share when contended (`min_r...

title: Add Audit Exporter Grafana dashboards + alert rules (lag, delivery errors)

This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to...
text: Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need first-class operational visibility for the audit-log-exporter service (lag, retries, adapter failures) and alerting aligned to the SLOs defined by SRE. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to hosted + dedicated environments for the beta cohort. Notes on semantics: Export lag is computed from exporter checkpoint watermark vs event time; alert...

title: Add runbook links to experiment alerts + Console guardrail banners
preview: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident s...
text: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident starts: alert payloads and the Console guardrail banners. This PR wires canonical runbook URLs into (1) alert payload annotations (so PagerDuty/Slack notifications carry a direct ‘Runbook’ link) and (2) Console guardrail banners on the experiment details/results pages. Scope: route experiments only (no global SRE alert conventions refactor in this PR). What changed: - Added a small, versioned mapping of {alert_key -> runbook_url} for experiment-related alerts (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant 0 traffic, allocation skew, p99 regression auto-stop). - Plumbed run...

title: Add Grafana dashboard: Route Experiment Guardrails (latency/error/cost + attribution drop)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds a standard Grafana dashboard for Route-level Experiments guardrails and data quality signals. The dashboard is intended to be deployed alongside the route experiments beta/GA so on-call and customers (where applicable) can quickly answer: (1) Is any variant regressing on latency, error rate, ...
text:
## Summary This PR adds a standard Grafana dashboard for Route-level Experiments guardrails and data quality signals. The dashboard is intended to be deployed alongside the route experiments beta/GA so on-call and customers (where applicable) can quickly answer: (1) Is any variant regressing on latency, error rate, or cost? (2) Are we missing experiment attribution (drop rate) such that results are untrustworthy? (3) Is traffic split behaving as expected (e.g., variant getting ~0% traffic)?

This dashboard complements the control-plane/Console views by providing a fast, infrastructure-centric view over raw telemetry, and is designed to work with the canonical attribution dimensions from ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region, model_id) while limiting cardinality blast-radius.

## What’s included ### Dashboard: "Route Experiments / Guardrails" Panels (high-level): - Overv...

title: Feature flag + cohort targeting for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics output
preview: Summary: Introduces a gated rollout mechanism for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics (deploy context/config diffs/saturation signals) so we can run a limited beta (SRE rotation + selected regions/channels) and safely roll back to v1 output if bot latency or upstream query cost spikes during incidents. Motivation: v2 diagno...
text: Summary: Introduces a gated rollout mechanism for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics (deploy context/config diffs/saturation signals) so we can run a limited beta (SRE rotation + selected regions/channels) and safely roll back to v1 output if bot latency or upstream query cost spikes during incidents. Motivation: v2 diagnostics increases upstream call volume and payload size; we need controlled exposure + a kill switch to avoid self-inflicted load during Sev events. Implementation details: Adds a new feature-flag abstraction (DiagV2Gate) with cohort targeting on Slack context (workspace, channel, user group), incident context (env/region/service), and optional allow/deny lists. Default behavior is OFF unless explicitly enabled; bot continues to render v1 cards with deep links. Adds three evaluation modes: (1) global enable/disable (INCIDENT_BOT_DIAG_V2_ENABLED), (2) cohort allowlist (INCIDEN...

title: Alerts: experiment config propagation lag + attribution anomalies (route experiments)
preview: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew ...
text: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew early in rollout). This PR adds Prometheus/Alertmanager rules and annotations for the standard route experiments dashboards, with SRE-reviewed runbook links and recommended severities. Scope: (1) propagation lag/staleness alerts tied to the new propagation lag metric emitted by the redwood monorepo, (2) attribution drop-rate and missing-dimension alerts for experiment metrics/traces aggregation, (3) “variant 0 traffic” detection to catch misconfigured allocations or eligibility rules. Notes: rules are written to avoid cardinality blow-ups (no per-route fanout by default); h...
